title: Group by Column (A.K.A. "Board Group by", "Dynamic Grouping", "Virtual Groups")

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
I don’t normally post here when a add a video to the Monday Man YouTube channel.

But, on this one, I thought most monday users will find this interesting. So, here you go:

Jim - The Monday Man (YouTube Channel)
Watch Our Latest Video: DYNAMIC GROUPING - The Most Powerful monday Feature Ever? - YouTube
Contact me directly here: Contact – The Monday Man

posts.author: CAllen (Carol Allen)
posts.cookedHtml: Very helpful. Thanks for this info.

posts.author: Stephden (Stephen Dennehy)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve been using this feature for about a month now and it is definitely a game changer. As someone who’s been using Monday.com for more than 18 months, my only real complaint with Monday.com is that you have to decide on how you want to group items when you set up a board. Using the “dynamic grouping” allows me to view my data in so many different and useful ways. I truly hope this alpha-level feature is carried through and is released as a standard feature. In the future, boards could be set up without grouping and then you could use the views to group data accordingly.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Stephden

I agree. It is a very useful feature. I especially like being able to temporarily “remove” groups.

posts.author: Kristinemd13 (Kristine Brooks)
posts.cookedHtml: Fingers crossed this makes it out of alpha!

posts.author: nicoferna (Nicolas Fernström)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
This looks like an interesting feature to try, but I don’t see it available in Monday labs as shown in the video. Has it been taken out of Alpha?

posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
Same here. I have these features on one of my accounts but when I tried to turn it on my other account I can’t find it on Monday.labs.

posts.cookedHtml:
@igorj79 & @nicoferna - It’s back in development for some revisions and improvements. Hopefully Group By will be back in monday labs soon. It’s super popular with our clients, everyone has loved it.

posts.cookedHtml: @JCorrell @PolishedGeek have either of you received any further information on when this will be re-released on either Alpha or Beta? This is a potential game changer for our use of Monday, hence keen for an update!

posts.cookedHtml: I agree! Do we have an idea of timeline for the release? Thank you.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@JustAnotherPM

Not sure how I missed replying to you.

I have not heard anything… It is still working for me on the accounts that I had previously activated it on. Really missing it on the new accounts though.

posts.author: klyntx (Karen D.)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 On this much needed feature. I was used to having the ability to Grpup By Column both an airtable and in Asana.

posts.author: Breackie (Frans Brakenhoff)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve was searching for this solution after the first quarter of this year. I was just too late to activate it and now I’m searching monthly for the reactivation of this function. It is not a nice to have, it is necessary above the current grouping function, which you then could solve with a (status) column in my opinion.

I just added my comment here, hopefully I then will be informed as soon as there is news about this amazing feature.

posts.author: Jorge (Jorge)
posts.cookedHtml: I can confirm that on a new account, this feature only appeared after activating a trial for the Dev product.

posts.author: ReturnOfTheMac (M-A C)
posts.cookedHtml: “Group by” was one of the reason why we decided to go with Monday. I feel a bit cheated that they have now removed it! Please Monday community managers, give us an ETA at least…

posts.author: johngcolejr (John Cole, Jr)
posts.cookedHtml: We need this option. bumping the thread

posts.author: rebeccaclay (Rebecca Clay)
posts.cookedHtml: Was so excited to find this option only to be disappointed it’s been removed! Definitely need this - please bring it back!

posts.author: kapardhichittella (Kapardhi Chittella)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

Any idea if this feature is back yet? or an estimated date when it is going to be back in Monday.labs?
I see it has almost been 8 months since it was taken out.

Any update is appreciated.
Thanks.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@kapardhichittella,

All I know right now is that monday has acknowledged the importance to the user community and that they are intending on bringing it back. The implication has been that it is much more complex on the back end that it seems and that they are wanting to make it a more complete feature. monday is usually VERY cagey about when features will be released. They have certainly cagey on this one. I do take the fact that they have even acknowledged it as a positive sign… how positive is anyone’s guess.

Jim - The Monday Man

posts.author: Dly215 (Duong )
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! I had to add their “dev” product, and then all the boards started having the “Group By” function. The function has stayed since the dev product trial expired.

posts.author: Krunchy (Guillaume Servonnat)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would be happy to have this group by functionality, it would make boards much more flexible and dynamic.

posts.author: Kmartucci (KMart)
posts.cookedHtml: Does the “dev” product a separate pricing plan? Or can anyone add it to the plan they are on? If the latter, how does one do that?

posts.author: Dly215 (Duong )
posts.cookedHtml: Dev is a paid product, but you can start a free trial (14 days I think), after which you won’t have access to it anymore. But as mentioned, you get to keep the “Group By” function even after the trial has ended.

posts.author: Kmartucci (KMart)
posts.cookedHtml: thank you!!! Game changing if this works.

posts.author: hollymyles (Holly Myles)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates here on when this will be implemented for all users?

posts.author: nietniek (nietniek)
posts.cookedHtml: 13 months later, and still hoping this function will be released soon, it removes a lot of workarounds, and makes boards more dynamic and easy to use.

posts.author: kslagerman (Keith Slagerman)
posts.cookedHtml: Our company has been disappointed to see so many features at Monday either hidden behind a pay wall or just absent. Dynamic grouping is a basic feature that is available with all of Monday’s competition. You actually accomplish dynamic grouping via automations now but that will count against your monthly automation “allowance” (ridiculous).

posts.author: ccy (ccy)
posts.cookedHtml: I was actually on SmartSuite till my organization decides to switch to Monday. The Group By function is a DEFAULT and BASIC function in SmartSuite… ;(

posts.author: PaulThePen (Paul)
posts.cookedHtml: Upvoted!!! This would be so helpful!

posts.author: hollymyles (Holly Myles)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update here?

posts.author: BogdanCodarcea (Bogdan Codarcea)
posts.cookedHtml:
I managed to obtain something similar through creating a Kanban view from a table and tick the Split View in the Kanban view which shows a table with dynamic grouping at the bottom and then if you create a new table view while still being in the Kanban View, the new table will have the same grouping. The problem is that I cant figure out how Monday.com is selecting the column on which the grouping is done and I cant select another one.

While still hoping for an update on this and the dynamic grouping to be added to the normal product, I must say that I`m quite dissapointed that it looks like different clients have different features available while paying for the same product. The only difference is activating something while it was in testing and then it remained in their account. Not to mention that the feature was made available in another product…

Hope that Monday.com team will realise that this is the kind of thing that make their clients getting annoyed and start looking at alternatives as it`s all about the way they approach the situation.

posts.author: BogdanCodarcea (Bogdan Codarcea)
posts.cookedHtml:
*Cards view not Kanban view

I managed to obtain something similar through creating a Cards view from a table and tick the Split View in the Cards view which shows a table with dynamic grouping at the bottom and then if you create a new table view while still being in the Cards View, the new table will have the same grouping.

posts.author: JoanRausch (Joan)
posts.cookedHtml: @BogdanCodarcea How do you tick the Split View. In Settings I can only see Cards Cover and Card Columns.

Hi Joan,

I have the split view option at the right bottom side of the page under the Display settings as highlighted in the image. Hope it helps
